description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from a series of large, blocky stone segments arranged in a semi-circular arch formation. The structure consists of two vertical piers, each made up of stacked rectangular blocks, supporting a curved archway composed of wedge-shaped voussoirs. The keystone at the apex of the arch is slightly larger and more prominent than the other stones, providing both structural integrity and visual emphasis. The surface of the blocks appears rough and unadorned, with no visible decorative reliefs, carvings, or inscription panels. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, evoking the style of ancient Roman or early medieval stonework. The archway is wide and tall enough to allow people or small vehicles to pass through comfortably.
description: A robust stone triumphal arch made of stacked rectangular and wedge-shaped blocks, featuring a prominent keystone and a rough, unadorned surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full archway, with stacked blocks forming the piers and a prominent keystone at the top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the blocks and the narrow profile of the arch structure.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the archway and block arrangement from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and depth of the stone blocks.
